other_observers: Bill Maynard and others; BPG found the bird
description: A very buffy Chordeiles nighthawk. Smaller in direct comparison to male Common Nighthawk (CONI). The wings were shorter and more rounded at the tip. Flight style was very different than the CONIs that were around. CONI was very jerky mechanical movement and the Lesser (LENI) was smoother and less jerky. When not flapping the LENI would glide with its wings in a fairly steep dihedral. The primary spot was very distal and small and buffy. The tail had even width bands down its length, alternating buffy and brown.Behaviors: Flying around treetop level feeding on flying insects. Numerous CONIs were around too allowing for great comparisons.Call: none notedPlumage: Definitive adult female
photographer: Photographer: Brian P Gibbons, Brian P Gibbons, Brian P Gibbons, Brian P Gibbons
